title: PHP Introduction
content:
### What is PHP?

PHP (Hypertext Preprocessor) is a widely-used open-source scripting language designed for web development. PHP scripts execute on the server and generate HTML sent to the client.

### Key Features

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| Server-side | Code runs on the server, not in the browser |
| Cross-platform | Runs on Windows, Linux, macOS |
| Database support | Works with MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite, and more |
| Embedded | Can be embedded directly into HTML |

### What Can PHP Do?

- Generate dynamic page content
- Create, read, write, and delete server files
- Collect form data
- Send and receive cookies
- Control user access
- Encrypt data

> **Key Point:** PHP is the backbone of many major CMS platforms like WordPress, Drupal, and Joomla.
